name: F-theory compactification
summary: F-theory (Vafa 1996) reformulates Type IIB string theory geometrically by treating the SL(2,Z) self-duality group as the diffeomorphism group of a torus fibred over the spacetime. Twelve-dimensional, with two extra dimensions on the auxiliary torus whose complex structure encodes the IIB axion-dilaton. On singular elliptic fibrations, 7-branes wrap divisors and exceptional gauge symmetries arise from Kodaira-singularity-type ADE classification. Provides a geometric framework for the most realistic local string-theoretic Standard Model constructions: F-theory GUTs (Beasley-Heckman-Vafa 2009) achieve gauge groups SU(5)/SO(10)/E_6 with three-generation matter and Yukawa hierarchies via geometric intersection patterns.

addresses_puzzles.note: Provides explicit large family of (semi-)realistic string vacua with controlled phenomenology. F-theory GUT constructions reproduce SU(5)/SO(10) gauge structure plus generation-three matter naturally; flux quanta count gives ~ 10^15-10^30 vacua, a more concrete landscape than abstract counting.

Yukawa hierarchies arise from geometric overlap of matter curves; rank-one matrix structure at GUT scale broken by flux/instanton corrections gives natural Froggatt-Nielsen-like patterns. Specifically reproduces m_t / m_b ~ tan(beta) and m_u / m_c / m_t ~ 1 / lambda^2 / lambda^4 structure.

Local F-theory GUTs achieve gauge-coupling unification through Kodaira-singularity gauge enhancement; doublet-triplet splitting and proton stability addressed by hypercharge flux mechanism (no need for missing-partner mechanism).

new_scales: compactification scale M_KK ~ 10^16 GeV (typical GUT scale)
introduces.note: Choice of elliptic fourfold, gauge-enhancement loci (matter curves), G_4 fluxes, and 7-brane configurations. Specific models reduce to a few effective parameters but the underlying choice space is the F-theory landscape.

history: Vafa (1996), 'Evidence for F-theory', Nucl. Phys. B 469, 403, arXiv:hep-th/9602022. Morrison-Vafa (1996), 'Compactifications of F-theory on Calabi-Yau threefolds I,II', hep-th/9602114 and hep-th/9603161 - geometric structure. F-theory GUTs revival: Donagi-Wijnholt (2008), arXiv:0802.2969; Beasley-Heckman-Vafa (2009), 'GUTs and Exceptional Branes in F-theory I,II', JHEP 01, 058 and 01, 059, arXiv:0802.3391 and 0806.0102. Modern reviews: Weigand (2010, arXiv:1009.3497); Heckman (2010, arXiv:1001.0577).

references.citation: Vafa, 'Evidence for F-theory', Nucl. Phys. B 469, 403 (1996)
Beasley, Heckman, Vafa, 'GUTs and Exceptional Branes in F-theory - I', JHEP 01, 058 (2009)
